reasoning: The control SC-36(6) focuses on the duplication of systems or system components in distributed locations, which is primarily a cloud infrastructure-level concern. Kubernetes itself does not inherently manage the geographic distribution or redundancy of clusters or nodes across multiple locations. This is typically handled by the underlying cloud provider or infrastructure layer, such as through multi-region or multi-zone deployments. Kubernetes can manage workloads within a cluster but does not natively handle cross-cluster or cross-region redundancy, which is necessary for this control.
title: Distributed Processing And Storage - Require the duplication of systems or system components in distributed locations
